extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates criminal, civil, statutory/regulatory, and tax matters and distinguishes “against” from “by” rows. I counted all “against” rows across the company, subsidiaries, promoters, directors, and KMPs/SMPs for criminal and regulatory categories: all are Nil, while tax proceedings against the company are 4 and those against subsidiaries are Nil; promoter tax proceedings (5) are excluded, and all “by” rows are excluded. The quantified group litigation amount is the company tax amount of ₹20.43 lakhs, with no quantified amount against subsidiaries, converted to ₹2.043 crore; promoter tax amount of ₹120.23 lakhs is excluded. The contingent-liabilities figure is the explicitly stated consolidated Fiscal 2025 aggregate of ₹19.18 lakhs as of March 31, 2025, converted to ₹1.918 crore, rather than a re-sum of disclosed items. The customer concentration figures are company-wide top-five and top-ten revenue from operations for full Fiscal 2025: 75.06% and 84.80%, respectively. The source monetary tables use lakhs, and conversion applied is ₹10 lakhs = ₹0.1 crore, or division by 100 for lakh amounts expressed in crore.
